question: What is the minimum attendance requirement for undergraduate courses?
answer: The minimum attendance requirement is ninety percent (90%) for each course after the add/drop period.

question: What happens if an undergraduate student fails to meet the 90% attendance requirement?
answer: A student who fails to fulfill this requirement without providing the approved supporting document may be barred from sitting the final examination.

question: When are undergraduate students allowed to add or drop courses?
answer: Students are allowed to add/drop courses within the first two (2) weeks of the semester.

question: When is the latest an undergraduate student can withdraw from a course?
answer: Students are allowed to withdraw from the courses latest by week six (6) of the semester.

question: What is the maximum duration of study for an Engineering Degree Programme?
answer: All Bachelor of Engineering students are required to complete their studies within seven (7) years.

question: What is the maximum duration of study for a Technology Degree Programme (e.g., Computer Science)?
answer: All Bachelor of Information Systems (Hons), Bachelor of Information Geology (Hons), and Bachelor of Computer Science (Hons) students are required to complete their studies within five and a half (5 1/2) years.

question: What is the minimum CGPA required for an undergraduate to graduate?
answer: Obtain a minimum Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 2.00.

question: What does the 'QF' grade mean?
answer: Qualified Fail (not obtaining 40% in coursework and final examination).

question: What is the minimum mark required in the final examination AND coursework to pass an undergraduate course?
answer: It is compulsory for a student to adhere to the policy of obtaining a minimum 40% of final examination and coursework mark for all courses in order to pass the course.

question: What CGPA range is required for a First Class Honours degree?
What CGPA range is required for a Second Class Upper Honours degree?
What CGPA range is required for a Second Class Lower Honours degree?
What CGPA range is required for a Third Class Honours degree?
What are the conditions for an undergraduate to get on the Dean's List?
answer: Must PASS all courses registered, must at least register a minimum of ten (10) credit hours, and must obtain a Grade Point Average (GPA) of 3.50 and above.

question: What CGPA range puts an undergraduate student on 'Academic Probation'?
answer: A student may be put on academic probation if he/she obtains a GPA or CGPA of 1.50 to 1.99 for any semester.

question: What CGPA range puts an undergraduate student on 'Academic Probation and Warning'?
answer: A student may be put on academic probation and warning if he/she obtains a GPA or CGPA of 1.00 to 1.49 for any semester or is under Academic Probation status for 2 consecutive semesters.

question: What is one condition for 'Academic Dismissal' for an undergraduate student?
answer: Being on academic probation and warning for two (2) consecutive semesters.

question: How long is the Student Industrial Internship Programme (SIIP)?
answer: The training is conducted over 28 weeks at relevant industries.

question: Is a deferred semester (with medical reason) counted as part of the period of study?
answer: Deferment with medical reason will not be considered as part of the period of study.

question: Is a deferred semester (without medical reason) counted as part of the period of study?
answer: Deferment without medical reason will be considered as part of the period of study.

question: What is the 'AU' symbol in an academic transcript?
answer: Audit course(s).

question: What is the 'W' symbol in an academic transcript?
answer: Withdrawal from course(s).

question: What is the 'SP' symbol in an academic transcript?
answer: Supplementary Pass.

question: Can an undergraduate student redeem (repeat) a course with a 'C' grade?
answer: The university has adopted the course redemption (grade replacement) policy for course/s with 'C' grade and below... for ONE (1) time replacement only per course.

question: If a student redeems a course, which grade is counted in the CGPA?
answer: Only the BEST grade will be counted in the total credit hours and cumulative grade point average (CGPA) calculation.

question: What is the maximum number of supplementary examinations a graduating undergraduate student can take?
answer: The supplementary examination is only applicable for a maximum of two (2) non-project-based course(s) in the semester.

question: What grade is recorded for a passed supplementary examination?
answer: Results for the Supplementary Examination shall be recorded as Pass (P) or Fail (F).

question: How long does a student have to appeal for remarking of a final exam?
answer: An appeal for remarking may be made within three (3) days after the results are released.

question: What is the fee for appealing for a remarking?
answer: Students are required to make a payment of RM100.00 (Ringgit Malaysia One Hundred Only) to the Finance and Fund Management Department for each course.

question: What is the maximum number of credit hours a full-time student can register for?
answer: All full-time students must register a minimum of nine (9) credit hours and maximum of sixteen (16) credit hours.
